name: Error Handling System
description: Unified error format with safe error exposure, debug-friendly structure, and specialized error types.

intent.what: Provides a hierarchy of error classes that normalize errors across the SDK with consistent codes, status codes, and structured details.
intent.why: Ensures consistent error handling, makes debugging easier, and allows consumers to handle specific error types.

steps: Throw appropriate error type in your code
Catch and handle based on error type/code
code_examples: throw new AuthError('Invalid credentials', { details: { userId: '123' } })

internal_flow: Constructor stores message, code, statusCode, details, timestamp, cause
toJSON() returns serialized version safe for API responses
Specialized classes set appropriate status codes
logic.error_hierarchy: NexoraError -> AuthError, DatabaseError, ValidationError, CacheError, RateLimitError, TimeoutError

anti_patterns: Don't expose raw Supabase errors directly
Don't include sensitive data in details
Don't use generic Error in SDK code

when_to_use: Use ValidationError for input validation failures
Use NexoraError.from() to convert unknown errors
when_not_to_use: Don't use for business logic errors
Don't throw generic Error in SDK code
reasoning_strategy: Identify error type (auth, validation, database, etc.)
Set cause to original error for chaining
